slate.do
GitHub Open Slate

CLI guide

Install Authenticate Basic commands Coding agents Upgrade Troubleshooting

Slate CLI

Your plan, available to every agent.

Install Slate from the terminal, connect your account, and let Claude Code, Codex, or any shell-based agent pull and update work.

Install in one line

The installer supports macOS and Linux on Intel and ARM. It downloads the matching release, verifies its SHA-256 checksum, and installs slate to ~/.local/bin.

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/owainlewis/slate.do/main/install.sh | sh

If needed, add the install directory to your path, then verify Slate:

export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
slate version
slate help
Prefer to inspect the script?

Open install.sh on GitHub before running it. The installer requires curl, tar, and either sha256sum or shasum.

Pin a release

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/owainlewis/slate.do/v1.0.0/install.sh | SLATE_VERSION=v1.0.0 sh

Set SLATE_INSTALL_DIR to install elsewhere. Windows users can use the Linux installer inside WSL.

Connect your account

  1. Sign in to Slate and open Settings.
  2. Under API tokens, create a token and copy it.
  3. Export it in the terminal where you will run Slate or start your agent.
export SLATE_API_TOKEN=slate_...
slate auth status
Keep the token private.

Do not put it in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, source code, or a committed .env file.

Find and manage work

Slate's data and workflow commands return JSON, including the IDs needed by later commands.

slate boards list
slate lists list --board <board-id>
slate tasks pull --limit 5
slate tasks get <task-id>

Create a task with enough context for an agent to execute it:

slate tasks create \
  --list <list-id> \
  --title "Draft launch note" \
  --description "Write the first version" \
  --date 2026-07-21

Run slate help boards, slate help lists, or slate help tasks for every command and flag.

Use Slate with Claude Code and Codex

Start the agent from the shell where the token is available. Use codex in place of claude for Codex.

export SLATE_API_TOKEN=slate_...
claude

Add this workflow to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md. Keep the token in the environment, not in the file.

## Slate workflow

- Run `slate tasks pull` to find queued work.
- Claim a task with `slate tasks claim <task-id>` before starting.
- Only continue when the claim succeeds.
- Read full context with `slate tasks get <task-id>`.
- When work is ready for review, run
  `slate tasks status <task-id> needs_review`.
- After acceptance, run `slate tasks done <task-id>`.
- Treat output as JSON and preserve IDs exactly.

Claims are atomic. If another agent already claimed the task, Slate rejects the second claim so the agent can select different work.

Upgrade or uninstall

Run the one-line installer again to upgrade. It only replaces the existing binary after checksum verification succeeds.

Remove a default installation with:

rm "$HOME/.local/bin/slate"

Troubleshooting

slate: command not found
Add ~/.local/bin to PATH, then open a new terminal.
SLATE_API_TOKEN is required
Create a token in Slate Settings and export it before starting the agent.
unauthorized
The token is missing, invalid, or revoked. Create a new one and run slate auth status.

See the v1.0.0 release for archives and checksums, or browse the latest release.
